across.clue: To be caught in the trap or damaged by the scheme you intended to use against others; to be the victim of your own plan or trick | be ___ by your own petard
Punish someone as a warning | Make an ___ of
Used to say that two choices or options are equally good, bad, or difficult; it makes no practical difference which you choose | it's as ___ as it's long
involved in an important way in making something happen.
A person cannot change their fundamental character, nature, or habits, no matter how hard they try | a ___ can't change its spots
to try to hold move or find something using your hands in a way that is not skilful or graceful.
To waste time and effort creating something that already exists; to do unnecessary work by starting from scratch | ___ the wheel
Having doubts about something that other people think is true or right.

down.clue: Clearly show an emotion or trait | Have something / be written all over your face
Not plan or control | Leave to ___
to officially get rid of a law system practice etc
to see or know something that will happen in the future.
to believe something is the cause of something else.
something with very unusual features that make it very different from other things of its type.
to make something less painful severe or serious
Something that seems bad or unfortunate at first but ultimately leads to a good or beneficial outcome | a ___ in disguise
